column1: # Public-Sector Evidence Taxonomy Routes (LIMEN Boost Shard 006)
| Evidence Type | LIMEN Category | Description | Jurisdiction Example |
| Procurement Contradiction | Institutional Absurdity | Conflicting requirements in public tenders | EU Digital infrastructure procurement rules vs. local data laws |
| Register Inconsistency | Institutional Absurdity | Contradictory entries in public registries | Estonian e-Residency vs. Tax Agency definitions |
| Overlapping Mandates | Governance Gap | Duplicate responsibilities across agencies | UK DCO vs. ICO AI oversight |
| Automated Decision-Making Without Review | Agentic Risk | Public service AI systems lacking human override | German Social Benefits AI|
| Self-Referential Legal Loops | Legal Contamination | Laws referencing themselves without closure | Spanish AI Regulation Article 12 circular reference |
- All routes validated against LIMEN core taxonomy (2026-06)
- Jurisdiction examples sourced from public registers (https://limen-registry.tyche.est)
- Prioritize evidence showing systemic gaps in public-sector AI governance
